name: subscriptions
context: Create and manage recurring billing with Stripe Subscriptions, prices, and invoices
info: Stripe Subscriptions automate recurring billing. A subscription ties a customer to one or more prices, generating invoices on a schedule. This skill covers creating subscriptions with trial periods, configuring products and prices (flat-rate, tiered, metered), handling upgrades and downgrades (proration), managing cancellations and pauses, and working with invoices. Subscriptions emit lifecycle events via webhooks for status changes, payment failures, and trial expirations.

sections.title: Create subscription
sections.body: Call `stripe.subscriptions.create()` with `customer`, `items` (array of `{ price }` objects), and optionally `trial_period_days`, `metadata`, and `default_payment_method`. The customer must have a payment method attached (see [[stripe/payments#payment-methods]]). Status starts as `active` or `trialing`. Listen for `customer.subscription.created` via [[stripe/webhooks]].

sections.title: Products and prices
sections.body: Create products with `stripe.products.create({ name, description })`. Create prices with `stripe.prices.create({ product, unit_amount, currency, recurring: { interval } })`. Intervals: `day`, `week`, `month`, `year`. Pricing models: flat-rate (`unit_amount`), tiered (`billing_scheme: 'tiered'`), metered (`usage_type: 'metered'`). Products can have multiple prices for different tiers or billing cycles.

sections.title: Trial periods
sections.body: Set `trial_period_days` on subscription creation for a simple trial. Use `trial_end` (Unix timestamp) for a specific end date. During trial, `status === 'trialing'` and no charges are made. Stripe sends `customer.subscription.trial_will_end` three days before expiry (see [[stripe/webhooks#event-types]]). After trial, the first invoice is generated and payment is attempted automatically.

sections.title: Upgrades and downgrades
sections.body: Update the subscription's `items` array with a new `price` ID to switch plans. Control proration with `proration_behavior`: `create_prorations` (default, credits unused time), `none` (no adjustment), `always_invoice` (invoice immediately). Retrieve the subscription first to get the item ID. For multi-item subscriptions, add or remove items individually.

sections.title: Cancellation
sections.body: Cancel at period end with `stripe.subscriptions.update(id, { cancel_at_period_end: true })` — the customer retains access until the current period expires. Cancel immediately with `stripe.subscriptions.cancel(id)`. Listen for `customer.subscription.deleted` via [[stripe/webhooks]] to revoke access and clean up resources. Pausing is also available via `pause_collection`.

scripts.name: Create Subscription
scripts.description: Create a Stripe subscription with customer, price, and optional trial period

STRIPE_SECRET_KEY.description: Stripe secret API key (sk_test_... or sk_live_...)

customer.description: Customer ID (cus_xxx) with an attached payment method

priceId.description: Price ID (price_xxx) for the subscription plan

trialDays.description: Number of trial days before first charge (0 for no trial)

metadata.description: Key-value metadata to attach to the subscription

status.description: Subscription status: trialing, active, past_due, canceled, unpaid

currentPeriodEnd.description: Unix timestamp of current billing period end

depends.reason: Requires a Stripe account and API key
Customer must have a payment method attached
